g.data-narration: Welcome to Image Segmentation Fundamentals. This comprehensive topic covers essential techniques for partitioning images into meaningful regions.
Image segmentation partitions a digital image into multiple segments by grouping pixels with similar characteristics like color, intensity, or texture.
Segmentation enables object detection, medical imaging for tumor detection, autonomous vehicle navigation, and content-based image retrieval systems.
g.text: • Medical imaging - tumor detection, organ segmentation
• Autonomous vehicles - road and obstacle detection

g.data-narration: There are two fundamental approaches: discontinuity-based segmentation detects abrupt intensity changes (edges), while similarity-based groups pixels with shared properties.
g.text: Detects edges - abrupt changes in intensity

g.data-narration: Edges are categorized by intensity transitions: step edges have abrupt changes, ramp edges transition gradually, roof edges rise and return, and line edges appear as thin lines.
First derivative operators like Sobel and Prewitt detect edges by computing intensity gradients. Sobel is more sensitive to diagonal edges due to larger weights on diagonal elements.
Second derivative operators like Laplacian detect edges at zero-crossings. The Laplacian of Gaussian (LoG) combines Gaussian smoothing with Laplacian for noise-robust edge detection.

The Canny edge detector is a multi-stage optimal edge detection algorithm with five key steps: noise reduction, gradient calculation, non-maximum suppression, double thresholding, and hysteresis tracking.
Step 1 applies Gaussian smoothing to reduce noise sensitivity. Step 2 computes gradients using Sobel operators for magnitude and direction. Step 3 performs non-maximum suppression to thin edges.
g.text: Canny Steps 1-3: Smoothing, Gradient, Non-Max Suppression
• Thin edges to single pixel width
Keep only local maxima in gradient direction

g.data-narration: Step 4 applies double thresholding to classify pixels as strong, weak, or non-relevant. Step 5 uses hysteresis to connect weak pixels to strong ones, forming continuous edges.
g.text: High Threshold (TH): Strong edges → kept | Low Threshold (TL): Weak edges → considered
Weak pixels connected to strong → kept | Weak pixels not connected → discarded

g.data-narration: Thresholding converts grayscale to binary using a threshold. Global thresholding uses one value for the entire image, while adaptive thresholding handles uneven illumination with local thresholds.
g.text: Otsu Formula: σ²B(w) = w₀(μ₀-μT)² + w₁(μ₁-μT)² - maximize this!

g.data-narration: Region growing starts from seed points and adds similar neighboring pixels. Region splitting divides non-homogeneous regions, while merging combines similar adjacent regions using quadtree decomposition.

Advanced methods include watershed algorithm treating images as topographic surfaces and K-means clustering partitioning pixels by feature space similarity. Evaluation uses accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score.
g.text: Image as topographic surface | Flood basins from markers | Merge at watershed lines
Initialize k centers | Assign pixels to nearest | Recompute centers | Repeat until convergence
Accuracy | Precision | Recall | F1-Score | Jaccard Index

g.data-narration: No single best segmentation method exists - the choice depends on application and image characteristics. Edge-based finds boundaries but may have gaps, while region-based produces connected regions.
g.text: • No single best method - depends on application and image characteristics
• Thresholding - fast and simple but limited to distinct intensity modes
• Edge-based - finds boundaries but may have gaps; must link edges
• Region-based - produces connected regions but sensitive to initialization
• Canny - optimal multi-stage edge detector with hysteresis
• Otsu - automatic threshold maximizing between-class variance

g.data-narration: For exams, understand differences between edge and region approaches, memorize Canny detector steps, practice Otsu calculations, and know when to apply each technique.
g.text: Exam Tips: Understand trade-offs | Memorize Canny steps | Practice Otsu calculations | Know applications
